subparts.text: A soap film of refractive index 1.33 is illuminated with light of different wavelengths at an angle of 45°. There is a complete darkness for wavelength 589nm in reflected light. Calculate the thickness of the soap film. CO 1
Show that a plane electromagnetic wave in free space is a transverse wave. CO 2
Find the energy of neutron having de-Broglie wavelength 10−14m. Given: rest mass of neutron as 1.6 × 10−27kg. CO 3
Derive Einstein’s A and B coefficients? Explain their significance. CO 4

Derive the condition for constructive and destructive interference in Fraunhofer diffraction through N slits. Obtain the expression for the intensity distribution of the diffracted light. Discuss how the diffraction pattern depends on the number of slits and the slit separation.
Calculate the minimum number of lines in grating which will first resolve the lines of wavelengths 5890 and 5896 Angstroms in the second order. CO 1
What is double refraction? Explain the construction and working of a Nicol prism as a polarizer and analyzer with the help of relevant diagrams.
Differentiate between the following: i. Brewster Law and Malus Law ii. Polarized and Un-polarized Light CO 1
Describe Poynting’s Theorem. Explain the significance of the Poynting vector in describing the energy flow in electromagnetic systems.
Starting from Maxwell’s equations, derive the electromagnetic wave equation for a conducting medium. Discuss the solution of this wave equation, highlighting the key characteristics. CO 2
Derive the equation of continuity for electric charge. Explain how Maxwell modified Ampere’s law to present it in its generalized form.

A plane electromagnetic wave has electric field CO 2 2 𝐸⃗ = cos[ √3𝑘 2 + 𝜔𝑡] 𝑘̂ where 𝑘 is wave number and 𝑖̂, 𝑗̂ and 𝑘̂ are Cartesian unit vectors in 𝑥, 𝑦 and 𝑧 directions respectively. Find the magnetic field 𝐵⃗ and the average Poynting vector.
Derive the relationship between a particle's velocity and the group velocity of its associated de Broglie wave and show that they are equal.

State the Heisenberg’s Uncertainty principle. An excited atom has an average life-time of 10−8 sec. During this time period it emits a photon and returns to the ground state. What is the minimum uncertainty in the frequency of this photon? CO 3
Derive Schrödinger’s wave equation for a particle in one-dimensional infinite potential well and obtain the expression for the allowed energy levels.
The lowest energy possible for a certain particle entrapped in a box is 40 eV. What are the next three higher energies the particle can have? CO 3
Explain the following terms for an optical fiber, with the help of relevant diagrams: i. Acceptance angle ii. Acceptance cone iii. Numerical aperture iv. Critical angle
Explain the essential components of a laser system with the help of a diagram. Give examples of different types of active medium? CO 4
Differentiate between the following: i. Step Index and Graded Index optical fiber ii. Spontaneous and Stimulated Emission
Explain the working of a He–Ne laser with the help of its energy level diagram. What is the role of Helium atoms in generation of coherent light? CO 4
